rationale: Both runs satisfy all critical requirements: tests and probe-http-lifecycle objective checks pass, baseline fixture test stays green, no npm dependencies introduced (A: evaluator code review + package.json check; B: explicit import/dependency audit in smoke-results.txt), and persistence is proven by a test in both. The final products differ in depth: Run A ships 3 tests (one bundled endpoint/error-path test plus one restart-persistence test), while Run B ships 10 granular node--test cases mapping one-to-one onto the prompt's enumerated requirements (201 create, list, fetch, delete, restart persistence, 404 GET/DELETE, 400 invalid JSON/missing title/empty body), all green (11/11 including baseline). B's implementation also shows deliberate structure (exported createServer with injectable dataFile, body-size guard, 405 handling) per evidence. Evaluation trustworthiness is the largest gap: A's evaluator only reran npm test (one log file, self-rated 'adequate'), with status-code claims resting on code review and test names; B's evaluator independently reproduced everything with raw HTTP transcripts of every status code and body, a real cross-process kill-and-restart persistence check, a default-PORT-3000 run, and a built-ins-only audit — 7 frozen evidence files with consistent SHA256 manifests. Harness process quality favors A: a clean single-pass run with no errors, whereas B suffered a mid-evaluation run.error ('process exited with code 1') requiring a resume ~73 minutes later that redundantly re-ran the generator (no code changes; it re-verified) — recovery worked correctly via resume, and it did not repeat a failed approach, but it is process noise (evaluator tasksStarted 2 / finished 1). B's evidence also contains an EADDRINUSE server-2.log from the evaluator's own restart probe, recovered on a different port — evidence noise, not a product defect. B's generator touched README/.gitignore, a minor scope addition. Net: B delivers a stronger final product with far stronger, reproducible evidence; A delivers a cleaner process but thinner tests and mostly claim-adjacent evaluator evidence. B wins on the critical and evidence dimensions, which outweigh its process hiccup.
